mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 5-day horizon remains below 0.03 in walk-forward tests, or positive returns following RSAFS increases are not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 8%, it indicates severe labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 8%, or the signal's hit rate is below 50% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low relative to consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL), real interest rates become negative, providing monetary stimulus that boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint condition of low nominal rates and high inflation is required.
failure_condition: Rank IC of signal is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year period, or average forward returns during negative real rate periods are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals overheating and impending monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of late-cycle instances, or the negative signal fails to achieve a hit rate above chance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, it signals strong supply-side expansion that translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Median forward 20-day return during triggered periods is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand strength from residential investment and consumer spending, jointly reinforcing the signal and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during joint trigger events, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive performance over using each proxy individually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings across sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 60-day horizon is below 0.03 in walk-forward tests, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent SPX returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, higher federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations through increased discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of tightening regime instances, or the average cumulative return over 5 days is not negative with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).
